title: fix: handle empty string model param in image generation
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: No model? No problem.
gist.blurb: Fixed a pesky bug where image generation would fail if you didn't explicitly specify a model. Now, if you leave it blank, we'll happily default to our trusty zimage model.
gist.summary: Fixed an API validation issue where empty model parameters caused image generation requests to fail.
gist.impact: Users can once again generate images without explicitly specifying a model flag in the CLI or API.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee handing a blank piece of paper (representing the empty parameter) to the Monitor Robot, who happily accepts it and displays a beautiful default image on its screen.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of a cute bee mascot with a tiny tool belt handing a blank piece of paper to a chunky robot with a CRT monitor head. The robot gives a thumbs up, its screen glowing with a beautiful generated landscape. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of a cute bee mascot with a tiny tool belt handing a blank piece of paper to a chunky robot with a CRT monitor head. The robot gives a thumbs up, its screen glowing with a beautiful generated landscape. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
